title: taraxacum pulverulentum
intro: Taraxacum pulverulentum, also known as hairy dandelion, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion genus Taraxacum, native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat digestive issues, due to its high content of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that promotes gut health. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving liver function, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Taraxacum pulverulentum can be consumed as a tea, used in tincture form, or added to salads and other dishes for its mild, slightly bitter flavor and potential health benefits.
